rag_findings.detail: The landscape and vegetation are highly characteristic of the Karoo or Northern Cape regions of South Africa. Furthermore, the specific design of the concrete utility poles on the right is a strong match for South African infrastructure, whereas Australian remote areas typically use different pole materials and designs.

The landscape features rocky outcrops and reddish sandy soil combined with xeric scrub that is more characteristic of the Namibian highlands than the flatter Kalahari basin of Botswana. Specialist evidence for botanics specifically notes that the rocky background is more typical of Namibia.

The utility poles on the right exhibit a specific tapered concrete design and cross-arm configuration that is strongly characteristic of South African infrastructure. While the arid landscape is common to both countries, the meta-evidence regarding the pole design and Street View coverage patterns points more definitively to South Africa.
